lesson: Lesson 1: The Origins of Boundary Law
caption: Timeline from English common law through American and Texas boundary law, marking livery of seisin, the four Texas sovereigns, and statehood (1845).

lesson: Lesson 2: How Boundaries Are Created
caption: Diagram contrasting a corner (the point) with the monument (iron pipe, brass cap, or natural feature) set at or near it.

lesson: Lesson 2: How Boundaries Are Created
caption: Map comparing the PLSS township-range grid (federal states) with a Texas GLO metes-and-bounds survey identified by abstract number.

lesson: Lesson 3: Estates and Interests in Land
caption: Tree diagram of estates in land: freehold (fee simple absolute, fee tail, defeasible fees, life estate) vs. non-freehold (leaseholds), with the matching future interests.

lesson: Lesson 4: Title, Ownership, and Possession
caption: Bundle-of-rights illustration: a parcel broken into separable sticks (surface, mineral, water, easement, lease) to show rights divided among holders.

lesson: Lesson 5: Deeds - Types, Covenants, and Requirements
caption: Annotated sample deed showing the standard clauses: recitals, granting clause/consideration, legal description with exceptions/reservations, habendum, and execution/acknowledgment.

homework: Homework 2 - Methods of boundary creation and the surveyor's role
caption: Reference diagram for the memo: the three methods of boundary creation with a small example sketch of each.

lesson: Sources and Hierarchy of Law
caption: Pyramid/hierarchy of legal authority: Constitution at top, then statutes, administrative rules, ordinances, with common/case law shown interpreting all levels.

lesson: The Federal and Texas Court Systems
caption: Side-by-side court hierarchy: federal (District -> Courts of Appeals -> U.S. Supreme Court) next to Texas (JP/Municipal/County/District -> Courts of Appeals -> the two high courts: Supreme Court of Texas and Court of Criminal Appeals).

lesson: How a Case Becomes Law: Precedent and Legal Research
caption: Flowchart: suit filed in trial court -> trial ruling (binds only this case) -> appeal on law/procedure -> appellate opinion (binds all courts in jurisdiction = precedent) -> supreme court review.

lesson: How a Case Becomes Law: Precedent and Legal Research
caption: Anatomy of a case citation labeled: style (parties), volume, reporter abbreviation (S.W.3d), page, and (court year) — using a Texas example.

lesson: Recording Acts, Notice, and the Bona Fide Purchaser
caption: Double-conveyance timeline: grantor sells to A (unrecorded), then to B (pays value, no notice); decision tree branching by race / notice / race-notice rules, highlighting the Texas notice outcome where B wins.

lesson: Presumptions and Inferences in Boundary Law
caption: Two-column comparison graphic: PRESUMPTION (imposed by law; rebuttable vs. conclusive) vs. INFERENCE (reasoned from facts), with a boundary example under each.

lesson: Presumptions and Inferences in Boundary Law
caption: Dignity-of-calls ladder showing natural monuments at top descending to quantity/area at bottom, with a note that intent and footsteps override the ladder.

lesson: What Is an Easement? Definition, Estates, and Classifications
caption: Dominant tenement (land benefiting) and servient tenement (land burdened) with the easement site straddling the servient parcel.

lesson: Creating Easements II: Implied, Necessity, Prescription, and Dedication
caption: A tract subdivided into a front and rear lot, with a driveway across the front serving the rear (back) lot — illustrating an implied easement by prior use / by necessity.

lesson: Related Concepts: Licenses, Encroachments, Covenants, Eminent Domain, and Reversion
caption: Reversion rights of lots along a vacated street: perpendicular to centerline on tangents, radial on curves, and by angle bisection at angle points.

homework: Homework 2 — Adverse Possession Track Selection + Easement Area Computation
caption: A 24-ft-wide access easement running 412.5 ft from a county road to a landlocked back lot, with dominant and servient parcels labeled.

lesson: Resolving Conflicts in Deeds: The Three Basic Elements
caption: A retracing surveyor finding an original iron pipe offset from a modern 'perfect' computed position, with an arrow showing the boundary held at the found monument (footsteps control).

lesson: Sequential vs. Simultaneous Conveyances
caption: Plan view of two sequential deeds sharing an overlap band, with the overlap shaded and labeled 'belongs to senior'; a separate gap band labeled 'belongs to seller / State'.

lesson: Sequential vs. Simultaneous Conveyances
caption: A platted block of equal lots between two found block corners, showing record total vs. measured total and the surplus distributed proportionally to each lot.

lesson: Senior and Junior Rights in Practice
caption: Frontage strip showing senior parcel A holding its full record width and junior parcel B reduced to the remainder, with the deficiency annotated on B.

lesson: The Dignity (Priority) of Conflicting Calls
caption: A vertical ladder/pyramid diagram of the dignity of calls from senior rights at the top down to coordinates at the bottom, with examples beside each rung.

lesson: The Dignity (Priority) of Conflicting Calls
caption: A line 'North 200.00 ft to a 2-inch iron pipe' where the found pipe is at 203.4 ft; arrow shows the corner held at the pipe (monument controls over distance).

lesson: Reading the Words: Rules of Construction, Parol Evidence, and Errors
caption: A deed traverse where one identifiable line's record bearing differs from its measured bearing by a fixed rotation; the same rotation applied to all other courses.

lesson: Possession as Evidence and the Surveyor's Responsibility
caption: A residential lot plat showing the deed/record line and an offset fence (line of occupation), both drawn, illustrating the surveyor's duty to disclose the gap rather than pick one.

caption: Frontage strip with senior width fixed and junior remainder spanning to the measured end monument

caption: A bearing line where the found monument sits beyond the record distance; corner held at the monument

template: Gap or overlap width between two adjoining deeds
caption: Two adjoining deed lines on a common baseline with the gap (or overlap) width dimensioned between them

caption: A single course from a found corner with the bearing angle, departure, and latitude triangle drawn to the next corner

caption: Plan of the original O tract subdivided into Parcels A, B, C along County Road 7, showing the two found road-corner monuments, the measured 592.40 ft frontage, and the found iron pipe on A's west line.

caption: Residential lot with the deed/record line and a parallel fence offset 1.6 ft inside it, dimensioned, to support the disclosure memo.

lesson: What Makes a Conveyance Simultaneous
caption: A recorded subdivision plat showing a parent parcel divided into numbered lots and blocks with streets, captioned to show all lots created at the same instant (equal dignity).

lesson: What Controls a Subdivision Boundary: The Order of Evidence
caption: A subdivision block where a found original iron pipe sits offset from the platted lot-line position, with the boundary held at the found monument (map yields to ground).

lesson: Single Proportionate Measurement: Apportioning a Block
caption: A block of six 100-ft record lots with the two original concrete end monuments found and a measured total of 596.44 ft, showing each lot corrected to 99.41 ft (record vs measured dimension strip).

lesson: Excess, Deficiency, Remnants, and Double Proportion
caption: A line between two found monuments with a lost interior corner, record distances a and b shown above and the proportioned position m·a/(a+b) shown below.

lesson: Excess, Deficiency, Remnants, and Double Proportion
caption: A PLSS-style lost section corner restored by double proportion: four surrounding found corners, with E-W and N-S proportioned positions converging on the restored corner.

lesson: Rules of Construction for Combined Descriptions
caption: A trapezoidal lot with non-parallel east and west side lines, showing the north–south dividing line splitting it into east and west halves, contrasted with a parallel-sided lot split on the mean bearing.

caption: Block of n equal record lots with the two original end monuments found and a measured total differing from record.

template: Cumulative proportion — distance to an interior lot corner
caption: Block with the corrected distance from a found end monument to an interior lot corner i lots in.

template: Excess/deficiency — total surplus and per-lot adjustment
caption: Record vs measured block total with the surplus/shortage labeled and distributed per lot.

template: Single-proportion restoration of a lost corner between two found corners
caption: Line between two found monuments with record distances a and b to a lost corner and the proportioned position below.

Six-lot block: top shows record 100-ft lots; middle shows the 596.44-ft measured span with only the two concrete end monuments; bottom shows the surviving cedar stake at the 4/5 corner at 395.23 ft / 201.21 ft.

lesson: Riparian vs. Littoral Rights and the Historical Roots of Water Law
caption: Side-by-side schematic: a riparian lot on a flowing stream vs. a littoral lot on a tidal shore, labeling the watercourse, the upland, and the differing boundary lines.

lesson: Riparian vs. Littoral Rights and the Historical Roots of Water Law
caption: Timeline of water-law sovereignty: Roman Civil Code, Siete Partidas (winter high water), English common law / De Jure Maris, U.S. equal-footing doctrine, and the four Texas sovereigns.

lesson: Navigability and Ownership of Beds: The Texas 30-Foot Statute
caption: Stream cross-section showing average bed width measured cut-bank to cut-bank, with a 30-ft dimension line and a note that the test ignores the day's water level.

lesson: Navigability and Ownership of Beds: The Texas 30-Foot Statute
caption: Two plan-view panels: navigable stream (State owns bed, private to gradient boundary) vs. non-navigable stream (owners to center/thread).

lesson: The Texas Gradient Boundary on Navigable Streams
caption: Cross-section of a navigable stream bank showing the lower water level at the foot of the cut bank, the higher non-overtopping level, and the gradient boundary drawn at the mid-height of the qualified cut bank; State bed below, private upland above.

lesson: The Texas Gradient Boundary on Navigable Streams
caption: Plan view of a non-navigable stream with two opposite owners holding to the medium filum aquae (centerline/thread).

lesson: Tidal and Coastal Boundaries: Tides, Datums, and the Dual Standard
caption: Beach profile showing MHHW, MHW, MLW, MLLW datum lines and the dry-beach zone, with the dual-rule note (MHHW for Spanish/Mexican grants, MHW for Texas grants).

lesson: Tidal and Coastal Boundaries: Tides, Datums, and the Dual Standard
caption: Before/after panels of a beach: gradual vegetation-line migration (easement rolls) vs. avulsive hurricane shift (easement does not automatically roll) per Severance v. Patterson.

lesson: Shoreline Change and Apportionment of New Land
caption: Two-panel diagram: gradual accretion moving the boundary outward (owner gains) vs. avulsive channel cut leaving the old boundary fixed and an island with its original owner.

lesson: Shoreline Change and Apportionment of New Land
caption: Riparian apportionment 'fan': several lots with old frontages on the original shoreline and proportional shares projected to the longer new shoreline.

lesson: Water USE Law: Surface Water Appropriation vs. Groundwater Rule of Capture
caption: Split schematic contrasting State-owned surface water allocated by TCEQ appropriation permits with landowner-owned groundwater governed by the rule of capture and GCDs.

template: Statutory navigability of a Texas stream from a vara width
caption: Stream bed cross-section with average cut-bank-to-cut-bank width labeled in varas and converted to feet, with the 30-ft threshold marked.

template: Apportioning new shoreline by proportionate-shoreline method
caption: Apportionment fan: three old frontages A, B, C on the original shoreline, with proportional shares projected outward to the longer new shoreline.

template: Area of accreted (riparian) land along a frontage
caption: Plan view of a riparian lot with its water frontage and the average outward depth of the accreted band, shaded as the new land.

caption: Fisherman Acres Subdivision plat: Lots A–E around Bass Bay, Trout Lake, Salmon River, and Bear Creek, with Pike Island in the river; lot lines call to the water features.

caption: Diagram of parcels A–G fronting a mix of water features (a navigable river, a non-navigable creek, a lake, a tidal bay/Gulf shore), each with a deed call to its water boundary.

caption: Side-by-side plan views of the same parcel: one annotated only with bearings and distances (metes), one annotated only with adjoiner/road/creek bounding calls (bounds).

lesson: Bearings, Azimuths, and the Body of Courses
caption: A single course drawn as the hypotenuse of a right triangle, with the latitude (vertical, D·cosβ) and departure (horizontal, D·sinβ) legs labeled and the bearing angle β marked from north.

lesson: Closure, Sufficiency, and Common Defects
caption: A four-course traverse drawn from the POB; the courses fail to return exactly to the POB, with the small gap dimensioned as the linear misclosure and ΣLat / ΣDep arrows shown.

lesson: Closure, Sufficiency, and Common Defects
caption: A vertical priority-of-calls ladder: natural monument at top, then artificial monument, adjoinder, course, distance, and quantity/area at the bottom, with intent and footsteps shown as overriding both sides.

lesson: Texas Conventions: Varas, the GLO, and Putting a Description Together
caption: A historic field-note course “S 45° E, 950 vrs” shown converting to 2,638.9 ft, with the 1 vara = 33⅓ in = 2.7778 ft scale bar.

lesson: Texas Conventions: Varas, the GLO, and Putting a Description Together
caption: A finished plat-and-description pairing: a labeled traverse with POC, POB, numbered courses, a curve, and bounding road right-of-way, beside the matching written Texas description with caption, area, basis of bearing, and seal block.
Compass rose with the four quadrants and the conversion rule Az = β / 180−β / 180+β / 360−β labeled by quadrant.

template: Latitude or departure of a single course
caption: A single course as the hypotenuse of a right triangle with latitude (D·cosβ) and departure (D·sinβ) legs labeled.

template: Area of a triangular parcel in acres
caption: A triangular parcel with its base along a road and the perpendicular height dimensioned, labeled with the area = (b×h)/2 formula.
